metric_value: More MIPS shipped in 1992 than any prior year — but least profitable year
notes: The mainframe paradox: volume up, profitability down

metric_name: IBM mainframe installed base decline rate
metric_value: Declining 7% per year as datacenters consolidate
notes: Continuous decline projected

metric_value: 30% vs 45% for PC-clone manufacturers
notes: Mainframe business less profitable than even PC-clone business

metric_value: IBM $30B; Microsoft $25B; Intel $25B; HP $20B
notes: Stock market has punished IBM for mainframe mistakes

metric_value: 18-36 months for major new applications; 6-9 months for trivial report changes
notes: Most critical issue for user organizations

metric_name: IS spending as share of capital spending
metric_value: Approximately 50% of all capital spending in many enterprises
notes: Yet mainframes viewed as cost center with negative returns

metric_value: IBM did not advance ES/9000 as it could or should; MVS closed; TCM fell behind RISC; DB2 trails by several generations; no RAID upgrade
notes: Aberdeen's hard-nosed assessment

metric_value: 12% increase in installations for 1992
notes: Growing in supposedly dying mainframe marketplace

metric_name: RISC vs mainframe TCM processor comparison
metric_value: High-end RISC now has power of largest mainframe TCM processors; next-gen RISC will handily outperform
notes: IBM announcing MVS port to RS/6000 as a result

metric_value: HP and NCR midrange: 20+ MB/sec per channel; IBM ESCOM mainframe: 9.5 MB/sec
notes: Midrange exceeds mainframe I/O; disk arrays standard on midrange, just introduced on mainframe

metric_value: Became a fiasco; IBM could not deliver promised services; killed mainframe ISV tools market
notes: Introduced 1990; intended to revitalize mainframe application marketplace

metric_value: DB2 does not have functionality of Oracle, Ingres, Sybase midrange competitors
notes: IS executives avoiding DB2 and IBM mainframes as a result

metric_value: No clear client-server path despite 1987 promises; did not fulfill vision
notes: Fundamental failure cited as reason IS managers moving away

metric_value: Annual fees increasing with no corresponding productivity gain; installed base declining creates destructive cycle
notes: CFOs targeting mainframe software as source of cost reduction

metric_value: Computer Associates pricing models exemplify trend to harvest mainframe business
notes: Largest independent mainframe ISV actively harvesting

metric_value: Majority of data center professionals are IBM-mainframe trained; 12-18 months to retrain even best staff
notes: Counter-veiling factor keeping mainframe alive; mainframe veterans unable to transition without boot-camp immersion

metric_name: Percentage of mainframe applications that are irrelevant
metric_value: IS managers report up to 80% of mainframe applications are now irrelevant to how enterprises do business
notes: Based on IS manager interviews; key finding supporting green-field approach

metric_value: More money spent on PCs than on mainframe computers
notes: IS managers wish to leverage PC investment through client-server

metric_name: Mainframe MIPS capacity growth despite decline
metric_value: Cheap mainframe CPUs allow datacenters to add 20-30% capacity per year for existing back-office apps
notes: Back-office apps last to be rehosted; ISVs actively rewriting for other environments

metric_value: SCAMP microprocessor, CCE software, rack-mount with industry-standard interfaces; Oracle, Unix server, PC LAN compatible
notes: Contrasted with IBM's closed approach; users maintain applications and skills while evolving

metric_value: IBM mainframes will be gradually phased out over next decade; probably irrelevant within 2-4 years
notes: Aberdeen projection based on user interviews

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
Three evolutionary paths: Surround (mainframe as data repository), Rehost/Convert, Rewrite
notes: Surround rated most realistic; rewrite rated least positive as it misses business process opportunity

metric_value: Availability of qualified internal personnel is typically the gating factor; nearly all require outside professional services
notes: Green-field begins with re-examining business processes; 2-3 year implementation horizon

metric_name: Aberdeen conclusion on IBM mainframe role
metric_value: Traditional IBM-defined mainframe role is merely maintenance in short term and irrelevant in long term
notes: Unless IBM makes major changes in same manner as Unisys

metric_value: Advanced midrange systems functionally more powerful than 95% of all installed mainframes for all applications including batch
notes: Midrange = multiuser; terminology shift predicted

metric_value: [UNVERIFIED]
Legacy COBOL mainframe applications must be completely rewritten for client-server; client/report/validation logic currently embedded in COBOL
notes: Objective of maximizing cheap desktop processing and minimizing expensive multiuser cycles

metric_value: New CEO interested in stock price performance; Aberdeen believes this portends disinvestment from mainframes
notes: Management playing Russian-roulette at headquarters; unlikely new executives will re-invigorate mainframe
